devopsExecution.commitNote: Combined 087+088 commit (50 files, 8690 insertions). 088's per-request --model override (modelswitch package) and 087's split-synthesis hunks co-mingle across agent.go/facade.go/config; a clean per-spec hunk-split was error-prone, so both ship intact in one commit. pii-scan clean; transient clients/.../.kotlin build artifact excluded.
devopsExecution.pushed: origin/main (10ed4a48..99c8d629); pre-push deploy-CLI uniformity lint PASSED; no --no-verify. The push also carried the pre-existing unpushed spec-085, spec-086, and framework-7.12.0 commits.
devopsExecution.ci: CI workflow (lint-and-test + cross-language-canary + build) GREEN — 088 Go validated on origin/main (agent-invoke model-override path + switchable-models allowlist + facade parity). build workflow: build-images ✓ (core+ML cosign keyless+Rekor signed, SBOM+SLSA attested, ghcr digest push), build-bundles (incl. home-lab) ✓. HISTORICAL NOTE (2026-06-14 dispatch): build-clients ✗ on a missing operator-private Android upload keystore secret then skipped publish-build-manifest. REFRESHED 2026-06-20: spec 098 (done) decoupled the mobile-client build from the server deploy manifest — on a non-release push build-clients is SKIPPED by design and publish-build-manifest tolerates that skip, publishing a server-only build-manifest; the ml Trivy CVEs were remediated (commits 4debc4f0 + d684f7bc). A re-run on current HEAD publishes the server-only manifest.
devopsExecution.deploy: blocked-on-operator — the server-only build-manifest now publishes (spec 098), so Build-Once Deploy-Many has a deploy input; the remaining blocker is the operator-owned bubbles.devops handoff (isolated push + CI + home-lab apply + live re-verify). The home-lab host is reachable (tailscale root ssh) + the deploy-adapter overlay adapter (smackerel/home-lab/) is present + cosign installed. MODEL TARGET REFRESHED 2026-06-21: the synthesis target deepseek-r1:7b is superseded — home-lab synthesis is now qwen3:30b-a3b and model selection is delegated to the deploy adapter params.yaml (commits 05b9f677 / 79e3d210 / ee174764); the effective synthesis model must be reconciled by the operator at apply time. Spec 089's 2026-06-15 live home-lab deploy (certifiedAt 2026-06-15T05:10:00Z, bubbles.devops) already carried 088's per-request --model override (switchable_models) and 087's synthesis split onto the live stack, so the live build is no longer pre-088 and the --model override is on the wire; the residual blocker is the operator-owned bubbles.devops apply of the current unpushed stack + the live GPU A/B re-verify against the operator-selected synthesis model.
devopsExecution.abTest: blocked-on-operator — THE DECISIVE TEST. Needs the 088 code deployed (so /v1/agent/invoke + Telegram /ask honor the model override) AND the operator-selected synthesis model resident (deepseek-r1:7b is superseded by the standing home-lab default qwen3:30b-a3b, delegated to the deploy adapter params.yaml; the live A/B target is the operator's apply-time choice). Cannot run a meaningful A/B against the pre-088 live stack. No A/B result captured (none fabricated).

devopsExecution.nextRequiredOwner: operator/user-session — re-run the build to publish the server-only build-manifest (now skip-tolerant of build-clients per spec 098; the Android upload keystore secret is only needed for a release-intent client build), deploy to home-lab, pull the operator-selected synthesis model (the standing home-lab default qwen3:30b-a3b, superseding deepseek-r1:7b), then run the gemma4:26b-vs-<synthesis-model> A/B on the pomegranate /ask turn.

executionHistory.summary: Greenfield analyst bootstrap. Grounded every requirement against committed architecture: verified the /ask model is a process-wide singleton (okagent.Config.Model + spec-087 SynthesisModel) baked at wiring (cmd/core/wiring_assistant_openknowledge.go) from SST (assistant.open_knowledge.llm_model_id / synthesis_model_id); confirmed BOTH surfaces converge on it (Telegram bot.go case ask -> Facade.Handle -> runOpenKnowledgeDirect -> CurrentAgent().Run; web/HTTP internal/api/agent_invoke.go POST /v1/agent/invoke -> substrate Handler -> agentRef.Load().Run); confirmed NO per-request/per-user/runtime override exists and NO general per-user prefs store (only per_user_token.go PASETO mint); confirmed the attribution gap (runOpenKnowledgeDirect does not set InvocationResult.Model; okagent.TurnResult + agenttool.outputEnvelope have no model field); confirmed allowlist SoT = config/smackerel.yaml model_memory_profiles + home-lab ollama_memory_limit 28G. Authored spec.md: problem statement, 6 actors, Outcome Contract (intent/success/failure-refusal/failure-condition), 8 BDD scenarios SCN-088-A01..A08, FR-1..FR-12, NFR-1..NFR-6, C1..C7 constraints, 3 design forks as open questions, Product Principle Alignment (Principle 8 primary + 6 + inherited 2/4), UI scenario matrix. No code written; no agents dispatched.

UX phase (parent-expanded). Read analyst spec.md + state.json, spec-087 uservalidation.md (house style), and the live assistant voice (render_outbound.go / render_openknowledge.go / contracts/refusal.go / legacy_aliases.go HelpText / bot.go command dispatch / api/agent_invoke.go envelope). Authored uservalidation.md (SCN-088-A01..A08 mapped to operator-checkable acceptance items, left unchecked [ ] per anti-fabrication since pre-implementation) and appended ## UI Wireframes + ## User Flows to spec.md. Defined 3 shared UI primitives for cross-surface parity (UX9): ModelOverrideCarrier, OverrideRejection, ModelAttribution. Proposed Telegram shapes (per-request /ask --model=<id>, sticky /model <id> + /model discovery + /model default, optional /compare) and API parity (top-level model field; success envelope adds model; off-allowlist/over-envelope => HTTP 400 with machine-readable error_code + structured rejected_model/allowed_models/default_model + a message identical to the Telegram sentence). Authored fail-loud rejection wording (names the model, double-NOT no-fallback, lists allowed set + default, actionable retry) and the '— model: <id>' attribution footer. Recommendation: attribute the human surface ONLY on override/compare (preserves spec-087 baseline byte-for-byte; Principle 6 / NFR-4); the API envelope carries model ALWAYS (structured metadata). UX input to Forks A/B/C recorded. No code; no agents dispatched; status unchanged. Next owner: bubbles.design.

Design phase (parent-expanded, from-analysis depth). Read spec.md (FR/NFR/SCN/forks + UI Wireframes/User Flows), uservalidation.md, and dependency 087 design.md. Grounded every integration point in committed code: confirmed the open-knowledge model is a process-wide singleton reached by TWO structurally-different fast-paths (Telegram Facade.runOpenKnowledgeDirect -> CurrentAgent().Run; web/HTTP agent_invoke.go fast-path -> CurrentAgent().Run BYPASSING the facade), so the shared validator+allowlist+clone MUST live at the agenttool singleton seam (not the facade). Verified per-turn reqModel selection (agent.go:332/342/394), the finalize() chokepoint, the attribution gap (TurnResult/outputEnvelope/InvocationResult carry no model on the fast-path), model_memory_profiles + OllamaMemoryLimitMiB on the main Config, and validateModelEnvelopes as the envelope seam. RESOLVED the 3 forks: A=per-request only (sticky deferred F-STICKY; no per-user store today); B=--model= re-points the SYNTHESIS turn only (tool-model deferred F-TOOLMODEL; re-pointing both would degrade gather on a reasoning model); C=defer compare-both (F-COMPARE-LATENCY; doubles worst case to 8400s busting the 4200s WriteTimeout; ask-twice enables A/B). Chose a NEW explicit assistant.open_knowledge.switchable_models SST list (fail-loud, co-residence-envelope-validated in validateModelEnvelopes) over reusing model_memory_profiles. Specified: carrier = typed contracts.AssistantMessage.ModelOverride (Telegram) + AgentInvokeRequest.Model (HTTP); per-request clone via new Agent.WithModelOverride (singleton never mutated, C6); shared modelswitch.Allowlist.Resolve validator returning typed Override/Rejection with two reason-codes (model_not_allowlisted / model_over_memory_envelope); attribution via new TurnResult.Model stamped in finalize -> outputEnvelope.model (HTTP always) + footer '— model: <id>' (Telegram only-on-override). 13-change file manifest + capability-foundation split + complexity tracking + trust-invariant matrix + scenario->test mapping authored. WriteTimeout unchanged (4200s). No code written; no agents dispatched; status unchanged (in_progress). Next owner: bubbles.plan.

Plan phase (parent-expanded). Read design.md (resolved Forks A/B/C + 13-change manifest), spec.md (FR/NFR/SCN-088-A01..A08), uservalidation.md, state.json, and spec 087 scopes.md + scenario-manifest.json (house style). Authored scopes.md: 3 sequential, scope-gated scopes mapping the design's 13 changes -> SCOPE-01 (SST switchable_models allowlist + shared modelswitch.Resolve validator, foundation:true, covers SCN-088-A02/A07; CHANGE 1-5), SCOPE-02 (AssistantMessage.ModelOverride carrier + Agent.WithModelOverride per-request clone [singleton never mutated, C6] + TurnResult.Model attribution + facade resolve/reject/thread, core spine, covers A01/A03/A04/A05/A08; CHANGE 6,7,8a,11), SCOPE-03 (Telegram --model= parse + footer, HTTP model field + 400 rejection envelope, wiring install, deploy contract, docs, concrete carriers, covers A06; CHANGE 8b,9,10,12). Each scope carries an Execution Outline, Gherkin (quoted from spec.md §4), a Test Plan (Gherkin->concrete test::function + category), and a tiered DoD (Tier-1 universal + Tier-2 role-specific), ALL checkboxes UNCHECKED (anti-fabrication; implementation pending). Authored scenario-manifest.json binding SCN-088-A01..A08 -> scopeId -> planned concrete test(s) with regressionRequired + evidenceRefs. Authored report.md skeleton (evidence-policy header + Change Manifest + per-scope PENDING + Test Evidence PENDING + Completion PENDING). artifact-lint PASSED (6/6 artifacts). WriteTimeout unchanged (4200s); compare-both deferred (F-COMPARE-LATENCY). Terminal posture validated-in-repo; no code; no agents dispatched; no commit (C7). Next owner: bubbles.implement.

policySnapshot.modelMatrixChanged: No NEW model added. Introduces a runtime, allowlist-gated, per-invocation model override over the EXISTING model_memory_profiles set (e.g. gemma4:26b / deepseek-r1:7b synthesis A/B; deepseek-r1:32b remains the operator opt-up). No ollama_memory_limit change; allowlist entries must stay envelope-consistent (each has a model_memory_profiles entry that fits the target env ollama envelope). A new SST key assistant.open_knowledge.switchable_models is a candidate allowlist mechanism (design fork — not yet decided).

planningOnlyJustification: Full-delivery greenfield spec that EXTENDS spec 087 (adds a runtime per-invocation model-override primitive; does not amend 087's closed artifacts). Planning packets from the analyst -> ux -> design -> plan prelude are materialised into spec.md / design.md / scopes.md / scenario-manifest.json. Implementation continues in the same spec under workflowMode=full-delivery; no separate implementation spec. Terminal posture is validated-in-repo: the live gemma4:26b-vs-deepseek-r1:7b A/B on home-lab hardware (the proof spec 087 could not run on dev) is a SEPARATE downstream bubbles.devops dispatch; this spec ships and validates the switchable-model primitive in-repo only (no commit/push here).

ownerDirective.summary: Make the LLM model used by the open-knowledge /ask agent runtime-switchable across surfaces so the operator can A/B-test models (e.g. gemma4:26b vs deepseek-r1:7b on the synthesis turn) WITHOUT redeploying. Owner: 'A/B testing is a plausible option, we always do that. Also maybe we should make models switchable — e.g., a Telegram command; other surfaces (web/API) should also have an api/option to do so.' An optional, allowlist-validated override is threaded transport -> facade -> per-invocation agent config (override Model and/or spec-087 SynthesisModel); no override == exactly today's SST baseline. Off-allowlist / envelope-busting model => explicit user-facing rejection listing the allowed set; never a silent default; never an un-profiled/over-envelope model reaching Ollama. Both Telegram and web/HTTP /ask surfaces get the capability, validated identically. The answer is attributed to the model that produced it. Preserve ALL spec-064/084/087 trust invariants under ANY model (cite-back, provenance/no-zero-source, capture-as-fallback inviolable, <think>-strip + retry-before-salvage). SST/NO-DEFAULTS/fail-loud (G028). Honour the spec-084/087 latency invariant. Do NOT touch spec-083 card-rewards WIP. Do NOT commit/push (devops owns the live A/B re-verify).
ownerDirective.dependencyChain: 064 -> 084 -> 087 -> 088 (087 is uncommitted, status blocked/validated-in-repo awaiting its own devops handoff; 088 depends directly on 087's SynthesisModel split and transitively on the 064/084 loop + trust perimeter).
designForks: Fork A — override scope/stickiness: per-request vs per-user-sticky vs global (global likely a non-goal; conflicts with SST/build-once).
Fork B — which turn(s) the override targets: tool-calling Model, spec-087 SynthesisModel, or both.
Fork C — first-class A/B compare affordance (one command runs both models) vs minimal switchable + ask-twice (interacts with the latency invariant).
